title: Ensure that the client certificate authorities file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the client certificate authorities file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the certificate authorities file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: The certificate authorities file controls the authorities used to validate API requests. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: The Client CA location for the kubelet is defined in /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf and is /etc/kubernetes/kubelet-ca.crt by default. Run the following command to view the user and group ownership: for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/kubelet- ca.crt done OR for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /host/etc/kubernetes/kubelet-ca.crt done Verify that the ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.default_value: By default, in OpenShift 4, the --client-ca-file is set to /etc/kubernetes/kubelet-ca.crt with ownership root:root . Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Step:** Connect to your OpenShift cluster using the IBM Cloud CLI.
   - Command: `ibmcloud oc cluster config --cluster <your-cluster-name>` (replace `<your-cluster-name>` with the name of your OpenShift cluster)
2. **Step:** Get the list of nodes in your OpenShift cluster.
   - Command: `oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'`
3. **Step:** For each node retrieved, check the ownership of the client certificate authorities file using the following command:
   - Command: `oc debug node/<node-name> -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/kubelet-ca.crt` (replace `<node-name>` with each node name obtained from the previous command)
4. **Step:** Validate the output of the previous command to ensure that the ownership is set to `root:root`.
5. **Expected Secure Configuration:** The output should show `root:root` for the ownership of the `/etc/kubernetes/kubelet-ca.crt` file.
6. **Confirm Compliance:** If all nodes return `root:root`, then the audit is compliant. If any node does not return this value, it is non-compliant and needs further investigation.
